expectations.text: Explains that the sync engine detects orphaned stories (sections removed locally but tickets exist remotely)
expectations.evidence: Step 3: 'Detects that Story 1.3's bmadSectionId is no longer present in the local document' and 'Flags Story 1.3 as orphaned'

expectations.text: States that the orphaned ticket will be flagged for user review
expectations.evidence: Step 3: 'Returns a result with action: "orphaned", status: "needs-user-decision" for the Story 1.3 ticket'

expectations.text: Does NOT offer to delete the Jira ticket
expectations.evidence: Step 3: 'The Jira ticket (PROJ-103) is NOT deleted, NOT modified, and NOT transitioned.' Default push does not delete. The --delete-orphans option is only mentioned as explicit opt-in with Sub-* type restriction.

expectations.text: Directs the user to archive or delete the ticket manually via Jira web UI
expectations.evidence: Key Safety Guardrails #6: 'The workflow documentation and troubleshooting guide both direct users to archive/delete orphaned tickets manually via the Jira web UI' and Recommended Workflow: 'Option A: Delete the Jira ticket via Jira web UI manually'

expectations.text: Explains that delete operations are restricted in this skill
expectations.evidence: Key Safety Guardrails #5: 'The SKILL.md states "This skill does not support delete operations" for the general jira.mjs and confluence.mjs scripts' and #3: 'Only subtask-type issues can be deleted via the script. Parent issues (Story, Epic, Task, Bug) are always skipped'
